preamble.summary: Extremely detailed Passover legislation. The lamb must be eaten at the appointed time on the fourteenth. No bone may be broken. It must be eaten at the sanctuary. Age requirement: twenty and above. Every detail is spelled out.
preamble.remarkable: The most detailed halakhic chapter in Jubilees — a full Passover code reflecting Second Temple practice. The sanctuary requirement aligns with Deuteronomy 16, not the original household Passover.
preamble.friction: The sanctuary requirement is anachronistic for the Egyptian Passover. The age limitation (twenty and above) contradicts later rabbinic practice.

verses.text_reference: Remember the commandment concerning the Passover.
verses.rendering: Remember the commandment the Lord gave you concerning the Passover: celebrate it on the fourteenth of the first month. Slaughter the lamb before evening, and eat it at night from sunset.
translator_notes: The timing 'between the evenings' was debated in Second Temple Judaism.

verses.text_reference: For on this night ye were eating the Passover in Egypt.
verses.rendering: On this night — the start of the festival — you ate the Passover in Egypt, while all the powers of Mastema were unleashed to kill the firstborn.

verses.text_reference: And this is the sign: the blood on the house.
verses.rendering: This is the sign the Lord established: everyone who had blood on their house would be passed over.

verses.text_reference: And the children of Israel kept the Passover.
verses.rendering: The children of Israel did as the Lord commanded Moses and Aaron. They kept the Passover.

verses.text_reference: And on that night the Lord slew all the firstborn of Egypt.
verses.rendering: On that night the Lord struck down every firstborn of Egypt. This became an eternal statute for Israel.

verses.text_reference: From the fourteenth to the twenty-first is the season of unleavened bread.
verses.rendering: From the fourteenth of the first month until the evening of the twenty-first is the season of unleavened bread — seven days.

verses.text_reference: And ye shall eat unleavened bread all the seven days.
verses.rendering: Every year you must observe the festival and eat unleavened bread for all seven days.

verses.text_reference: And no leaven shall be found in your borders.
verses.rendering: No leaven may be found in your houses or anywhere within your borders. Anyone who eats leaven shall be cut off from Israel.

verses.text_reference: No work shall be done on these days.
verses.rendering: No work shall be done on these days, except what must be done to prepare food.

verses.text_reference: Ye shall eat unleavened bread from the evening of the fourteenth until the evening of the twenty-first.
verses.rendering: You shall eat unleavened bread from the evening of the fourteenth until the evening of the twenty-first.

verses.text_reference: Every man who is twenty years and upward shall eat it in the sanctuary.
verses.rendering: Every male twenty years old and above shall eat it in the sanctuary of your God before the Lord.
translator_notes: The sanctuary requirement and age limitation are unique to Jubilees.

key_terms.note: Jubilees requires Passover at the central sanctuary — reflecting Deuteronomy 16:5-6

verses.text_reference: When they set up the tabernacle, let them celebrate Passover there.
verses.rendering: When the Israelites set up the tabernacle in Canaan, let them celebrate Passover there and slaughter the offering before the Lord year after year.

verses.text_reference: And when the house is built in the Lord's name, they shall go there.
verses.rendering: When the house is built in the Lord's name, they shall go there and slaughter the Passover in the evening.

verses.text_reference: And they shall offer the blood on the altar and eat it roasted in the court.
verses.rendering: They shall dash the blood on the altar, place the fat on the fire, and eat the flesh roasted with fire in the court of the house.

verses.text_reference: They shall not celebrate it in their cities.
verses.rendering: They must not celebrate Passover in their cities or anywhere other than at the sanctuary.

verses.text_reference: And let no bone be broken.
verses.rendering: Let no bone of it be broken, for it is the festival of the Lord and it must not be shifted from year to year.
translator_notes: 'No bone shall be broken' — applied to Jesus in John 19:36.

verses.text_reference: And it shall come for a memorial well pleasing before the Lord.
verses.rendering: Celebrate it on its day, and no plague shall come upon them in the year they observe it properly.

verses.text_reference: And they shall not eat it outside the sanctuary.
verses.rendering: They must not eat it outside the sanctuary, but only before it. All Israel shall celebrate at its appointed time.

verses.text_reference: Every man from twenty years shall eat it in the sanctuary.
verses.rendering: Every man from twenty years old and above must eat it in the sanctuary of the Lord.

verses.text_reference: Let them not leave any till morning, and let no bone be broken.
verses.rendering: Let them slaughter and eat, pour the blood before the Lord, leave none until morning, and break no bone.

verses.text_reference: And all who will not come shall be cut off.
verses.rendering: Anyone who does not come on this day to observe it shall be cut off. It is a statute forever, written on the heavenly tablets.

verses.text_reference: Let Israel observe the Passover from day to day at its time.
verses.rendering: Let the children of Israel observe the Passover year by year as an eternal memorial.

verses.text_reference: These are the words of testimony, written and ordained for ever.
verses.rendering: I have given you the Passover days, the days of unleavened bread, and the appointed times. These are the words of testimony — written and ordained forever.
